title: EFT: Focusing
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides for focusing in EFT.

sources.label: Watson and Sharbanee, Emotion Focused Therapy: Case Conceptualization and Treatment

slides.title: Marker: felt, not yet symbolized
slides.body: Use focusing when a vague bodily sense needs attention before action.
bullets: The client says: I do not know; it is hard to name.
There is a bodily referent: throat, chest, stomach, breath.
The issue is too global for chair work, too alive for advice.

slides.title: Focusing micro-sequence
slides.body: Keep the pace below the client's thinking speed. Focusing works when the body has time to answer.
steps: Clear enough space: set aside the story, not the client.
Invite attention into the bodily middle and wait for the whole feel.
Ask for a handle: word, image, gesture, texture, temperature, movement.
Check resonance: does that word fit it, or is something off?
Ask from contact: what is the worst of this, or what does it need?
Receive the shift and let the new meaning settle before moving on.

slides.title: Protect the felt sense
slides.body: The therapist's discipline is to keep attention with the implicit edge without turning it into analysis.
items: Reflect images exactly; do not improve them.
Check whether the body receives each phrase.
If analysis takes over, return to body.
